seoTitle: How to Record Rent Payments in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record rent payments in QuickBooks Online, whether you're paying rent as a business expense or receiving it as a landlord.
intro: Rent shows up in QuickBooks Online differently depending on whether you're the one paying it or the one collecting it. This guide covers how to record rent payments accurately in either scenario.

steps.heading: Determine your role in the transaction
steps.body: Decide whether you're recording rent as an expense you pay or as income you receive from a tenant.

steps.heading: Set up the relevant account
steps.body: Confirm you have a rent expense account, or a rental income account if you're the landlord.

steps.heading: Enter the transaction
steps.body: Record the rent payment or receipt, assigning it to the appropriate account and linking it to the correct customer or vendor.

steps.heading: Set up recurring transactions if needed
steps.body: If rent is paid or received on a regular schedule, consider setting up a recurring transaction to save time.

faq.question: Is rent a deductible business expense?
faq.answer: Rent for business space is generally a deductible expense, but confirm the specifics with your accountant.

faq.question: Can I automate recurring rent payments?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online supports recurring transactions, which is useful for a fixed monthly rent amount.

faq.question: How do I record rent received as a landlord?
faq.answer: Rent received should be recorded as income tied to the tenant, similar to receiving a customer payment.

faq.question: What if rent includes utilities or other charges?
faq.answer: You may want to split the payment into separate line items if it includes charges beyond base rent.
